content_en:
Abu Ishaq Ibrahim ibn Musa ibn Muhammad al-Lakhmi al-Gharnati ash-Shatibi was born in Granada (Arabic: Gharnata) in the middle of the eighth century AH (fourteenth century CE). The exact date of his birth is not recorded with certainty. He spent his entire scholarly life in Granada, which was at that time the last remaining significant Muslim political entity on the Iberian Peninsula — a society under permanent military pressure from the Christian kingdoms of Castile and Aragon, but one that continued to sustain a rich intellectual and cultural life.

Ash-Shatibi studied under the leading scholars of Granada and Andalusia, receiving training in Arabic linguistics, Maliki jurisprudence, hadith, and theology. He was associated particularly with the great grammarian Abu Ali al-Manshuri, who taught him the Basran approach to Arabic grammar that would significantly influence his later legal methodology. His deep knowledge of Arabic linguistics informed his approach to the interpretation of Islamic legal texts: he understood legal language as a form of natural Arabic usage and insisted that legal interpretation must be grounded in a thorough understanding of how the language actually functioned.

His scholarly career was not without controversy. His insistence on grounding Islamic legal practice in the Quran and Sunnah, and his criticism of popular religious practices that he considered innovations without sound basis, brought him into conflict with some of the established religious authorities of Granada. His willingness to offer independent legal judgments also attracted criticism from those who felt he was departing from the established positions of the Maliki school. These controversies did not diminish his scholarship; if anything, they sharpened his argument for a principled approach to legal methodology.

He died in Granada in 790 AH (1388 CE), leaving behind al-Muwafaqat as his most systematic work and al-I'tisam, a study of bid'ah (innovation), as his most polemically significant text.
content_id:
Abu Ishaq Ibrahim ibn Musa ibn Muhammad al-Lakhmi al-Gharnati al-Syathibi lahir di Granada (bahasa Arab: Gharnathah) pada pertengahan abad kedelapan Hijriah (abad keempat belas Masehi). Tanggal pasti kelahirannya tidak tercatat dengan pasti. Ia menghabiskan seluruh kehidupan keilmuannya di Granada, yang saat itu merupakan entitas politik Muslim terakhir yang signifikan di Semenanjung Iberia — masyarakat yang berada di bawah tekanan militer permanen dari kerajaan-kerajaan Kristen Castile dan Aragon, namun tetap mempertahankan kehidupan intelektual dan budaya yang kaya.

Al-Syathibi belajar kepada para ulama terkemuka Granada dan Andalusia, mendapat pelatihan dalam linguistik Arab, fikih Maliki, hadis, dan teologi. Ia berafiliasi khususnya dengan ahli tata bahasa besar Abu Ali al-Mansyuri, yang mengajarinya pendekatan Basri terhadap tata bahasa Arab yang akan secara signifikan mempengaruhi metodologi hukumnya di kemudian hari. Pengetahuannya yang mendalam tentang linguistik Arab membentuk pendekatannya terhadap penafsiran teks-teks hukum Islam: ia memahami bahasa hukum sebagai bentuk penggunaan bahasa Arab yang alamiah dan menegaskan bahwa penafsiran hukum harus didasarkan pada pemahaman mendalam tentang bagaimana bahasa itu sesungguhnya berfungsi.

Karier keilmuannya tidak lepas dari kontroversi. Penekanannya pada pengakaran praktik hukum Islam dalam Al-Qur'an dan Sunnah, serta kritiknya terhadap praktik-praktik keagamaan populer yang ia anggap sebagai bid'ah tanpa dasar yang kuat, membawanya ke dalam konflik dengan sebagian otoritas keagamaan mapan di Granada. Kesediaannya memberikan penilaian hukum independen juga menarik kritik dari mereka yang merasa ia menyimpang dari posisi-posisi mapan mazhab Maliki. Kontroversi-kontroversi ini tidak mengurangi kualitas keilmuannya; bahkan sebaliknya, kontroversi itu mempertajam argumennya untuk pendekatan berprinsip terhadap metodologi hukum.

Ia wafat di Granada pada 790 H (1388 M), meninggalkan al-Muwafaqat sebagai karya paling sistematisnya dan al-I'tisham, sebuah kajian tentang bid'ah (inovasi), sebagai teks paling signifikan dari segi polemiknya.
